name: HMS Spurs (FY 168)
events: 2 Jul 1940 HMS H 49 (Lt. M.A. Langley, RN) shifted from Harwich to Dover. According to the log of HMS H 49 she was escorted by HMS Skipjack. But HMS Skipjack was lost on 1 June 1940 so this can't be correct.
Also according to the log of HMS H 49, HMS Spurs (T/Lt. R.S. Connell, RNR) took over the escort at 1800 hours. (1)

15 Apr 1944 HMS Ursula (Lt. A.G. Davies, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Methill, first with HMS Westminster (Lt.Cdr. H.G. Bowerman, DSC, RN) and HMS Valorous (Lt. M.G. Macleod, RN) and later with HMS Preston North End (T/Lt. D.K. Bennett, DSC, RNVR), HMS Spurs (A/Skr.Lt. E.J. Day, RNR) and HMS Arab (T/Lt. J.S.H. Lloyd, RNVR).
On completion of the exercises HMS Ursula proceeded to Rosyth. (2)

18 Apr 1944 HMS Ursula (Lt. A.G. Davies, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Methill, first with HMS Castleton (Lt. S.E. Jagger, RN) and HMS Ludlow (T/Lt. J.A. Graham, RNVR), later with HMS Polka (T/Lt. I.A. Thorpe, MBE, RNVR) and HMS Leicester City (T/Lt. C.E. Irving, RNVR) and finally with HMS Valse (T/Lt. E.J. Newell, RNVR) and HMS Spurs (A/Skr.Lt. E.J. Day, RNR). (2)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
